fields.action: South African documents apostilled by DIRCO (Department of International Relations and Cooperation). South African Police Service (SAPS) clearance, apostilled by DIRCO. Have non-German documents translated by a certified translator. Prepare two complete sets — originals and copies.

fields.name: Employer secures Federal Employment Agency approval
fields.why: For most work visas the Bundesagentur für Arbeit must approve the role, confirming the pay matches local standards. EU Blue Card shortage roles often skip the priority check, which is why the Blue Card is faster.
fields.need: Your employer completes the "Erklärung zum Beschäftigungsverhältnis" declaration. You provide passport details and contract.
fields.action: Handled by your employer and the embassy in the background once you apply. Confirm your HR has the declaration form ready — embassy delays often trace to a missing employer declaration.
fields.risks: A slow or unresponsive employer stalls the whole visa. Below-norm salaries can trigger rejection. This runs in parallel with your embassy appointment.
fields.doneWhen: The Federal Employment Agency has issued approval (confirmed internally during processing).

fields.name: Get your qualifications recognised (anabin / ZAB)
fields.why: Germany only grants skilled worker visas if your qualification is recognised as equivalent to a German one. This is the most common reason applications stall — verifying recognition first prevents wasted months.
fields.need: Your degree certificates and the anabin database (anabin.kmk.org). A ZAB Statement of Comparability costs around 200 EUR if formal evaluation is needed.
fields.action: South African degrees checked on anabin; generally well regarded. English-medium education is an advantage. Search anabin first; if your institution is rated H+ recognition is automatic, otherwise request a ZAB Statement of Comparability.
fields.risks: Regulated professions (doctors, nurses, teachers, lawyers) follow a separate, longer recognition process. Lesser-known universities often need full ZAB evaluation. Start this step first — it gates everything else.
fields.doneWhen: Your institution shows H+ on anabin, or you hold a ZAB Statement of Comparability.

fields.name: Convert to a residence permit at the Ausländerbehörde
fields.why: Your entry D-visa is temporary. You convert it into an electronic residence permit (eAT) or EU Blue Card at the local foreigners authority — your long-term right to stay.
fields.need: Passport with visa, Anmeldung certificate, employment contract, health insurance proof, biometric photo, and the fee (~100 EUR).
fields.action: Book the Ausländerbehörde appointment as soon as you have your Anmeldung — these are the most backed-up offices in Germany. You may receive a Fiktionsbescheinigung (interim certificate) while the card is produced.
fields.risks: Waiting times of 2-3 months are normal in major cities. Letting your entry visa expire before the appointment creates legal complications. Statutory health insurance must be active by now.
fields.doneWhen: You hold your residence permit / EU Blue Card (eAT), or a Fiktionsbescheinigung confirming continued legal stay.

fields.name: Book and attend your visa appointment
fields.why: Appointment slots at German missions are limited and can be booked out for weeks. Your consular jurisdiction depends on where you live — you cannot freely choose.
fields.need: The embassy booking portal, your passport, the visa fee (75 EUR), and your complete document set.
fields.action: Book the National Visa (D-visa) for employment as early as possible. German missions in South Africa: Pretoria, Cape Town. Attend in person, submit documents, and give biometrics.
fields.risks: Booking in the wrong jurisdiction means rejection on the day. Peak-season waits (4-12 weeks) mean book the moment you have an offer. Third-party "fast appointment" agents are often scams.
fields.doneWhen: You have attended your appointment, submitted documents and biometrics, and hold a tracking reference.

fields.action: Apply via Make-it-in-Germany.com, LinkedIn, StepStone.de, and Xing. Be explicit that you need visa sponsorship. English accepted in international roles; German strengthens applications.
